personality_one_liner: A tactical, military-grade command center for logistics tracking with high-contrast readouts and absolute zero decorative friction.

spacing.rule: Dense, grid-like alignment. Padding is functional and tight. Minimal whitespace to maximize telemetry visibility.

dont's: No blur or glassmorphism. User explicitly requested 'no soft gradients' and a tactical command center feel. Use solid surface tones and borders for elevation.

screens.name: Launcher / Login
screens.purpose: Secure authentication ensuring single-device session per driver.

states.loading: Centered monospace 'SYNCING...' with small activity indicator.
states.error: Red tactical alert box 'SESSION ACTIVE ON ANOTHER DEVICE'.

screens.layout: High-contrast login form. Monospace labels above inputs. Solid #007AFF primary button pinned to keyboard. A status text at the bottom 'SafeDrive Enforcer Active'.

screens.name: Driving HUD
screens.purpose: Live telemetry view with route map, speed, next turn, and panic trigger.

states.loading: Acquiring GPS Signal... (Blinking yellow warning status)
states.error: GPS Offline - Switching to Black Box Burst Mode

screens.layout: Top-third: Active Trip ID & ETA. Middle: Minimal dark map with high-contrast route line. Lower-third: Oversized monospace current speed (km/h) + Next instruction pill. Bottom edge: 'Windshield Reflection' toggle and a critical red 'Silent Panic' button. When Reflection Mode is ON, apply a full-screen vertical flip (scaleY: -1) and maximize contrast to reflect correctly on the windshield at night.

screens.name: Secure Chat
screens.purpose: Dispatch communication with speed-based input locking.
screens.layout: Standard chat feed but tactical style (sharp bubbles, mono timestamps). Bottom input area: If vehicle speed > 5 km/h, the text input is fully DISABLED and replaced by a horizontal scroll of 4-5 Quick Reply chips (e.g., 'Copy', 'Delayed', 'At Checkpoint'). Full keyboard unlocks only at 0 km/h.

screens.name: Alerts & Black Box
screens.purpose: Logs route deviations and displays offline data queue.
screens.layout: Vertical list of log rows. Each row: mono timestamp, colored status badge (e.g., Deviation [Red], Offline Log [Gray]), and detail text. Shows a persistent progress bar at the top if offline data is waiting for a 'Burst Sync'.

instructions_to_main_agent: STRICT ADHERENCE TO DARK MODE: Only use the defined tactical color palette (#050505 base, #121212 surfaces, high-contrast text). No light mode variants.

WINDSHIELD REFLECTION MODE: Implement a toggle on the HUD screen that wraps the main view in a container with `transform: [{ scaleY: -1 }]` (or X depending on the physical dashboard placement preference, usually Y for windshield reflection). This is critical for the night driving requirement.

SPEED-LOCKED CHAT: In the Secure Chat screen, simulate a speed state. If `speed > 5`, disable the TextInput completely and only render a horizontal ScrollView of `Pressable` quick-reply chips. This is a core safety requirement.

NO GLASSMORPHISM / BLUR: The user explicitly requested 'no soft gradients'. Use sharp edges (border-radius: 4 to 8), solid background colors, and border lines (`border` or `borderStrong`) to define visual hierarchy instead of shadows or blur.
TYPOGRAPHY: Use 'IBM Plex Mono' for all telemetry data (speed, coordinates, ETAs, timestamps) and 'IBM Plex Sans' for descriptive UI text. This fulfills the military-grade command center aesthetic.

SILENT PANIC BUTTON: Must be a highly visible button (using the `#FF2A2A` critical color) located on the main HUD. Simulating a 'Screen Off' state can be done by rendering a full black absolute overlay when triggered.
